summary.text: Historical data is provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence. Real-time stock price data is provided by CBOE and 15-minute delayed consolidated tape pricing data is provided by Nasdaq UTP. Several fund data points are also provided by Finnhub.

overview.description: The Columbia Large Cap Growth ETF (REGS) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in large cap equity. The fund is actively managed, seeking capital appreciation through investments in US large-cap stocks. The selection process combines fundamental analysis with signals like price momentum and investor sentiment. REGS was launched on Nov 20, 1992 and is issued by Columbia Threadneedle.

holdings.infoBox: REGS is an equity ETF with a total of 65 individual holdings. The top holdings are NVIDIA stock at 11.28%, Apple at 8.52%, Broadcom at 6.86%, Alphabet at 5.59%, and Meta Platforms at 4.43%.

summary.pre: ETF holdings data is provided by
summary.post: . Holdings are typically published by the ETF issuer at the end of each trading day.
